purpose: Audit the Wave 1-11 repair/build workflow, top-level notes, plans, validation rules, build protocol, and current VBTPro pipeline waves to identify missing or drifted details before starting Wave 04A implementation.

scope.baseline_workflow: Waves 1-11 validator-led repair chain and closeout workflow
scope.current_wave_chain: Full VBTPro private research pipeline Wave 02-08 roadmap with Wave 04A as the next implementation slice

wave_1_11_baseline.current_adaptation: Current VBTPro waves should copy the cadence and proof boundary of Waves 1-11, with one additional requirement: full-detail VBTPro source/read evidence before implementing code that depends on exact VBTPro runtime surfaces.

current_strengths: Durable turn protocol now captures Build Mode, compact slice packets, conditional index refresh, and validator-led wave proof.
Wave 04A packet names the owning validator and focused test to be created during implementation.
Build-wave protocol validator enforces compact packet shape, owning validator/test inclusion, VBT read references, and stale index-refresh wording.
Wave 04A packet quarantines future placeholders so GPU ML, paper trading, LLM agents, and NVIDIA optional profiles do not leak into the loader slice.

findings.evidence: The private_vbtpro_research_pipeline route lists the roadmap, Wave 02 packet, loader semantics plan, research notes, and full-read policy/register, but does not list plans/v7_2_5/vbtprobuild_wave_04a_full_read_packet_20260514.json or the build-wave protocol validator command.
findings.impact: Agents using the guidance map alone can find the pipeline roadmap but may miss the exact Wave 04A compact packet and start-gate validator.
findings.recommendation: Add the Wave 04A packet and build-wave validator command to the private_vbtpro_research_pipeline route or a child route before Wave 04A implementation.

findings.evidence: The read-only harness still covers Wave 1-11 repair validators and tests, but does not include scripts/v2_validate_build_wave_protocol.py in summary-only/repo-corpus command groups or its focused pytest.
findings.impact: The new start-gate validator is proven directly, but the reusable read-only harness does not yet protect it from future accidental writes.
findings.recommendation: Add the build-wave protocol validator and focused test to the read-only harness policy or schedule this in the next harness-maintenance slice.

findings.evidence: The script surface usage policy active allowlist does not include scripts/v2_validate_build_wave_protocol.py, and helper/catalog discovery does not expose it until index/catalog refresh or reconciliation.
findings.impact: The validator works when called directly from the packet, but script-selection discovery can under-report the new validator.
findings.recommendation: Register the build-wave validator in the script surface policy/catalog during the next reconciliation or index-refresh maintenance step.

findings.evidence: Wave 02 and Wave 03 objects still contain stale 'new validator/test' wording after those files were implemented. Wave 03 exact commands include the Massive OHLCV writer validator, but the required_validators list still centers the source-backed profile validator and omits the writer validator.
findings.impact: The roadmap is usable because exact commands and later protocol language are stronger, but duplicate sections can mislead future packet creation.
findings.recommendation: Normalize implemented-wave language and make Wave 03 required_validators match the actual owner validators already used.

findings.evidence: The Wave 04 object still has descriptor-era owner surfaces while the current implementation order splits Wave 04 into Wave 04A loader/feature semantics, Wave 03 provider-completion bridge, and Wave 04B real-output loading.
findings.impact: A direct reader of the Wave 04 object could start descriptor/real-output work too early instead of using the Wave 04A packet.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit Wave 04A and Wave 04B owner-surface blocks or revise the Wave 04 object to route through the Wave 04A packet first.

findings.evidence: Wave 05-08 objects rely on the future-wave template for the build-wave start gate instead of listing scripts/v2_validate_build_wave_protocol.py directly inside each wave's required_validators.
findings.impact: Future packet validation will catch missing start gates, but individual wave objects are less self-contained.
findings.recommendation: When each future packet is created, copy the build-wave start-gate validator into that wave's explicit required validator list.

findings.evidence: Top-level rules mention compact slice packets and focused validators/tests. The durable protocol has the exact owning-validator/owning-test requirement, but SKILL.md does not spell out those fields as strongly as the protocol.
findings.impact: Agents reading only top-level prose may understand the packet requirement but miss the exact owning validator/test naming rule until they read the durable protocol.
findings.recommendation: Add one concise phrase to SKILL.md or AGENTS.md that every implementation packet must name the owning wave validator and owning focused test.

findings.evidence: The active plan set still points to the completed crack-repair execution plan as current active plan authority, while the VBTPro research pipeline roadmap is routed through the guidance map instead.
findings.impact: This is structurally acceptable, but agents using only the active plan output can land on post-Wave-11 closeout rather than the current VBTPro build route.
findings.recommendation: Either leave as-is and rely on the guidance map, or add a lightweight active-route note that the crack repair chain is closed and current private VBTPro pipeline build work is routed through the private_vbtpro_research_pipeline guidance layer.

findings.evidence: The roadmap validation_matrix for the roadmap artifact does not include scripts/v2_validate_build_wave_protocol.py even though the roadmap now depends on that validator for build-mode packet proof.
findings.impact: Roadmap validation can pass while omitting the validator that proves future packet readiness.
findings.recommendation: Add the build-wave protocol validator to the roadmap artifact validation matrix.

findings.evidence: The validator has explicit future-boundary keyword checks for Wave 04A, but future-wave-specific not_in_scope expectations are not config-driven yet.
findings.impact: Wave 04A is protected, but Waves 05-08 will need their own packet-specific boundary checks when activated.
findings.recommendation: When creating each future packet, either extend the validator with that wave's boundary keywords or move wave-specific boundary expectations into a small config-owned policy.

findings.evidence: The code-change authority query does not directly return the durable turn protocol. AGENTS.md still requires reading it before detailed planning or protected edits.
findings.impact: No practical blocker if AGENTS.md is followed, but a tool-only authority query does not surface the build-mode protocol by itself.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding the durable turn protocol to the production authority index for code_change scope during the next authority-index maintenance pass.

strongest_pre_wave04a_cleanup: Add Wave 04A packet and build-wave validator lookup to the private VBTPro pipeline guidance route.
Add build-wave protocol validator to the roadmap validation matrix.
Normalize Wave 04A/04B routing in the roadmap so direct readers do not skip the loader slice.
safe_to_defer: Read-only harness inclusion for build-wave protocol validator.

conclusion.wave04a_condition: Use the Wave 04A compact packet and build-wave protocol validator as the start gate.
conclusion.summary: The current build workflow correctly copies the Wave 1-11 validator-led cadence and adds the necessary VBTPro full-read gate. The remaining issues are small routing, discoverability, and duplicate-section drift details, not architecture blockers.
